headline: Anthropic's Fable 5 launch dominated the month as users contested its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8.
body: Anthropic's Fable 5 (also branded Mythos 5) drove the window with live evaluations, benchmarks against GPT 5.5, and capability demos spanning 3D reconstruction, ray tracing, and medical imaging, while a $200-plan user reported the model burned 45% of a weekly quota in a single day. Anthropic separately reversed a policy that would have quietly degraded Claude on frontier LLM development tasks, conceding it mistraded transparency for safeguards. On the hardware side, Groq detailed its LPU inference chip and NVIDIA shipped Nemotron 3 in Nano, Super, and Ultra tiers built on a hybrid Mamba-Transformer with latent mixture of experts. Among techniques, tool calling, MLLM-as-judge, GRPO, and embodiment-centric generation all weakened while long-horizon planning and attribution broke out and GPU acceleration and auto-scaling entered as new.

cited_items.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
